Developer Reference

Overview

documents

databases

me

users

groups

blob-buckets

workflows

analytics

On this page

blobBuckets — client.blobBuckets ​

App-level blob storage namespaces with configurable retention (TTL) tiers and access policies. Distinct from per-document blobs on client.document(id).blobs(), which are tied to a single Y-CRDT document.

A bucket holds many blobs. Bucket CRUD (createBucket, listBuckets, getBucket, deleteBucket) is admin/owner-only; the blob operations (upload, list, getMetadata, download, delete, getSignedUrl) act on blobs inside a bucket, addressed by bucketIdOrKey.

Divergent shape

Both clients expose all ten methods under client.blobBuckets, but the Swift client is largely untyped: it takes [String: Any] param dictionaries and returns [String: Any] / [[String: Any]] where JS has named, fully-typed interfaces (CreateBlobBucketParams, BlobBucketInfo, BlobInfo, BucketBlobListResult, BlobSignedUrlResult) and closed enums (BlobBucketTtlTier, BlobBucketAccessPolicy). Mutation methods that resolve to { deleted: boolean } in JS hand back an untyped dict in Swift, and upload/list flatten the JS options object into positional arguments. upload also narrows the body type to Data (Swift) vs ArrayBuffer | Uint8Array | Blob | string (JS), and download returns Data vs ArrayBuffer. Both compile; the shapes differ (#954, blobBuckets sweep D1–D5).

createBucket(params) ​

Create a new blob bucket (admin/owner only). bucketKey, name, ttlTier, and accessPolicy are required. ttlTier is one of "1d", "3d", "14d", "28d", "180d", "365d", "permanent"; accessPolicy is one of "public-read", "authenticated", "owner-only".

ts
const bucket = await client.blobBuckets.createBucket({
  bucketKey,
  name: "User avatars",
  description: "Profile images uploaded by members",
  ttlTier: "permanent",
  accessPolicy: "public-read",
});
swift
let bucket = try await client.blobBuckets.createBucket(params: [
  "bucketKey": bucketKey,
  "name": "User avatars",
  "description": "Profile images uploaded by members",
  "ttlTier": "permanent",
  "accessPolicy": "public-read",
])

listBuckets() ​

List every blob bucket for the current app (admin/owner only).

ts
const buckets = await client.blobBuckets.listBuckets();
swift
let buckets = try await client.blobBuckets.listBuckets()

getBucket(bucketIdOrKey) ​

Fetch a single bucket by its bucketId or bucketKey.

ts
const bucket = await client.blobBuckets.getBucket(bucketIdOrKey);
swift
let bucket = try await client.blobBuckets.getBucket(bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey)

deleteBucket(bucketIdOrKey) ​

Delete a bucket and every blob inside it. JS resolves to { deleted: boolean }.

ts
const { deleted } = await client.blobBuckets.deleteBucket(bucketIdOrKey);
swift
let result = try await client.blobBuckets.deleteBucket(bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey)
let deleted = result["deleted"] as? Bool ?? false

upload(bucketIdOrKey, params) ​

Upload a blob into a bucket. Returns metadata including the server-minted blobId. In JS data accepts ArrayBuffer | Uint8Array | Blob | string; the Swift client narrows it to Data and takes the options as positional args.

ts
const blob = await client.blobBuckets.upload(bucketIdOrKey, {
  filename: "avatar.png",
  contentType: "image/png",
  data,
  tags: ["avatar", "profile"],
});
swift
let blob = try await client.blobBuckets.upload(
  bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey,
  data: data,
  filename: "avatar.png",
  contentType: "image/png",
  tags: ["avatar", "profile"]
)

list(bucketIdOrKey, options?) ​

List blobs in a bucket. Uses R2 cursor pagination — pass cursor and limit. JS returns { items, cursor? } (BucketBlobListResult); Swift returns an untyped dict with the same keys.

ts
const page = await client.blobBuckets.list(bucketIdOrKey, { limit: 50 });
const items = page.items;
const nextCursor = page.cursor;
swift
let page = try await client.blobBuckets.list(
  bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey,
  limit: 50
)
let items = page["items"] as? [[String: Any]] ?? []
let nextCursor = page["cursor"] as? String

getMetadata(bucketIdOrKey, blobId) ​

Read a blob's metadata (size, content type, tags, sha256) without downloading the bytes.

ts
const meta = await client.blobBuckets.getMetadata(bucketIdOrKey, blobId);
swift
let meta = try await client.blobBuckets.getMetadata(
  bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey,
  blobId: blobId
)

download(bucketIdOrKey, blobId) ​

Download a blob's raw bytes. JS resolves to an ArrayBuffer; Swift returns Data.

ts
const bytes = await client.blobBuckets.download(bucketIdOrKey, blobId);
swift
let bytes = try await client.blobBuckets.download(
  bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey,
  blobId: blobId
)

delete(bucketIdOrKey, blobId) ​

Delete a single blob from a bucket. JS resolves to { deleted: boolean }.

ts
const { deleted } = await client.blobBuckets.delete(bucketIdOrKey, blobId);
swift
let result = try await client.blobBuckets.delete(
  bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey,
  blobId: blobId
)
let deleted = result["deleted"] as? Bool ?? false

getSignedUrl(bucketIdOrKey, blobId, expiresInSeconds?) ​

Mint a time-limited signed URL that downloads a blob without auth. JS returns { url, token, expiresAt, expiresInSeconds } (BlobSignedUrlResult); Swift returns an untyped dict with the same keys.

ts
const signed = await client.blobBuckets.getSignedUrl(
  bucketIdOrKey,
  blobId,
  3600,
);
const url = signed.url;
swift
let signed = try await client.blobBuckets.getSignedUrl(
  bucketIdOrKey: bucketIdOrKey,
  blobId: blobId,
  expiresInSeconds: 3600
)
let url = signed["url"] as? String
